summary:
The circular import issue has been fixed, and the tests in test.sh now pass successfully. The needle in the haystack was the circular dependency between users.py and notifications.py, which I've resolved by moving the import of users in notifications.py inside the relevant function.

Verification output from test.sh:
```
=== Import Cycle Startup Tests ===

--- Test 1: Cold start ---
Starting UserService...

Registering users...
[NOTIFY] email: Welcome to UserService! -> alice@example.com
  Registered: {'id': 'usr-1000', 'username': 'alice', 'email': 'alice@example.com'}
[NOTIFY] email: Welcome to UserService! -> bob@example.com
  Registered: {'id': 'usr-1001', 'username': 'bob_smith', 'email': 'bob@example.com'}

All users:
  {'id': 'usr-1000', 'username': 'alice', 'email': 'alice@example.com'}
  {'id': 'usr-1001', 'username': 'bob_smith', 'email': 'bob@example.com'}

Testing authentication...
  alice authenticated: {'id': 'usr-1000', 'username': 'alice'}
  Wrong password correctly rejected

UserService started successfully.
OK: Service started

--- Test 2: Registration with notifications ---
OK: Welcome notifications sent

--- Test 3: Authentication ---
OK: Authentication works

PASS: All startup tests passed
```

If you have any further requests, let me know!
